response.justification: Bogdan is a highly experienced web and backend engineer, but this role requires deep, sustained C++ expertise for industrial automation systems, which he fundamentally lacks. His C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a university thesis project — far short of the 8–12 years demanded. His primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js, and he has no experience with industrial protocols (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus), IEC 62443 security standards, or the IoT/embedded domain central to this role.
strengths: Strong event-driven and distributed systems design experience, including a self-built event-driven GIS platform and micro-services work at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests per second.
Demonstrated Agile, TDD, and software craftsmanship discipline across multiple senior and staff-level roles.
Cross-domain engineering breadth (embedded, mobile, web, DevOps) shows adaptability and a solid CS foundation.
concerns: C++ experience is negligible — approximately six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and one student project; it does not appear in his listed skills at all.
Zero exposure to industrial automation, IEC 62443, SDL practices, MQTT, OPC UA, or any of the industrial protocols listed as core or advantageous requirements.
Currently in an active career gap since October 2025, and his most recent work is in TypeScript/AI tooling, moving further from the C++ systems domain rather than toward it.
